rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
Are you interested in movies, music, or YouTube in English? What is the relationship between poetry and hip hop?
By looking at different elements of popular culture this course allows for the development of English skills in a realistic and entertaining setting.
aims: This is a four skills (reading, writing, speaking, listening) English language course. Lessons are related to the theme of popular culture and will introduce a variety of useful English skills including vocabulary, grammar constructions, thematic writing, and creative expression. The skills which are introduced will be practiced in two student projects.
goals: Students will learn English grammar and vocabulary
Students will learn creative and expressive techniques in English
Students will learn about popular culture through materials in English
Students will engage with and describe English language media and culture

plan.text:
[Week 1. Course introduction]
Introduction and overview of the course, assessments and other requirements.

[Week 1. Course introduction]
Introduction of the class blog and how to use this

[Week 2. Patterns in English - Root words]
English is composed of repeated patterns that we can use to help us understand the meaning of words. This lesson looks at Greek and Latin root words that are used in English.

[Week 2. Patterns in English - Affixes]
Affixes are attached to the front or back of English words. Understanding affixes can help us quickly understand the meaning of English words.

[Week 3. Online media in English - YouTube]
This lesson introduces some popular YouTubers and YouTube channels.

[Week 3. Online media in English - Series]
This lesson introduces some different series (such as sci-fi, comedy, animation, and adventure) that are available on online media platforms.

[Week 4. Popular Culture - References]
The popular culture is composed of the things we do, experience, and consume. This lesson will look at how the way that language is central to popular culture.

[Week 4. Popular Culture - Slang]
Slang are neologisms (new words) which are invented to allow new ways of speaking and expression.

[Week 5. Project 1 - Introduce the project]
This lesson will introduce our first project "Introduce a YouTube channel, series, or movie" and its two components: a presentation and a language checklist.

[Week 5. Project 1 - Popularity and trends]
Students will use two online tools for checking the popularity over time and will consider reasons why trends fall in or out of popularity.

[Week 6. Project 1 preparation - Making a PowerPoint presentation]
This lesson will introduce skills for making an effective PowerPoint presentation. Students will work on their PowerPoint presentations.

[Week 6. Project 1 preparation - Practice and feedback]
Students will practice their presentation with the teacher and get individual feedback on this with the goal of improving their performance.

[Week 7. Project 1 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.
Students will also submit their language checklists which showcase their knowledge of the content learned in Weeks 2, 3 and 4.

[Week 7. Project 1 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.
Students will also submit their language checklists which showcase their knowledge of the content learned in Weeks 2, 3 and 4.

[Week 8. Poetry - Syllables]
Syllables are the sound beats inside words. This lesson will look at syllables using the short Japanese form of poetry -- haiku.

[Week 8. Poetry - Simile and metaphor]
This lesson will look at image techniques that are used to make meaning in poetry.

[Week 9. Poetry - Rhyme]
Rhyming words sound alike. Rhyming sounds pleasant and this technique is used in poems, songs, advertising and more. Let's explore!

[Week 9. Poetry - Limerick]
In this lesson, we will continue on the topic of rhyming by learning about the short rhyming poetry called limericks.

[Week 10. Hip Hop - History]
Although they might look completely different, poetry and hip hop are related. This lesson will give a short history of the music genre of hip hop.

[Week 10. Hip Hop - Themes]
Hip hop is a street culture which represents the lives and struggle of minorities and people in challenging situations. This lesson will look at the major ideas and themes in hip hop.

[Week 11. Hip hop sound - Sound techniques]
This lesson will look at some of the distinctive sound elements such as scratching, rapping and beat that feature in hip hop.

[Week 11. Hip hop sound - Listening to hip hop]
In this lesson we will listen to excerpts of hip hop music and consider the techniques and themes within.

[Week 12. Hip hop language - Slang]
This lesson will look at the language of hip hop and how and why it is used.

[Week 12. Hip hop language - Lyrics]
In this lesson, we will look deeply at the lyrics of a hip hop song and analyse what these mean.

[Week 13. Project 2 - Introduce the project]
This lesson will introduce our second project "Discuss a poem or song deeply" and its two components: a presentation and a language checklist.

[Week 13. Project 2 - Author background]
The background of an author has a strong influence on the work they produce. This lesson will provide skills for researching and writing about the background of an author.

[Week 14. Project 2 preparation - Timelines]
Timelines can be used to summarise a series of historical events. They are an effective way to present information within a presentation. Students will learn how to make and present a timeline.

[Week 14. Project 2 preparation - Presentation practice and feedback]
Students will practice their presentation in pairs and with the teacher and get feedback on this with the goal of improving their performance.

[Week 15 - Project 2 presentation]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.
Students will also submit their language checklists which showcase their knowledge of the content learned in Weeks 8,9,10,11, and 12.

[Week 15. Project 2 presentation]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.

[Week 16. Final test]
Students will review course content in preparation for a classroom test.
[Week 16. Final mini test]

textbooks: Materials will be provided in class
prereq:
-Students should have an interest in popular culture including the course areas of poetry, hip hop, YouTube, and movies
-Students should be willing to participate in class activities including spoken activities and presentations.
-Students must complete any online class content including participating in the class blog.
-Students must submit all assessments, including spoken presentations, essays, blog posts, etc., before the end of the last lesson (unless there is a written agreement with the teacher stating that the student can submit at a later date). If all assessment tasks are not completed, the student will not be able to pass the course.
-Students must not be absent for more than 1/3 of total classes.
prep: It is necessary to complete homework and preview/review lesson content.
teachers: ◎ マガシック ミケランジェロ

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
30% Project 1 (Presentation + language checklist = 20+10) PowerPoint presentation with a focus on practiced speech, competency in the learned material.
30% Project 2 (Presentation + language checklist = 20+10) PowerPoint presentation with a focus on practiced speech, competency in the learned material.
20% Class blog/Participation = Attendance and attitude, completion of activities and homework,
20% Final mini-test = Review of content

その他
Class materials are available on Moodle and should be previewed before class and reviewed after.
The teacher will introduce the class materials briefly.
Following which, students will complete activities at their own pace. Unfinished activities should be completed as homework.
